title: Twice-Baked Tasty Peruvian Potatoes Causa Limena
description: Comforting yeasty pillowy Peruvian dish featuring jersey royal potatoes and nimono-simmer, low-sodium-friendly.
instructions: Pierce the potatoes several times with a fork to allow steam to escape, and bake for 45 minutes, or until tender.
While the potatoes are baking, prepare the nimono-simmer by sautéing the chopped onion in butter until softened.
Add the chicken broth to the pan and bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and cook for 10 minutes.
Once the potatoes are done, let them cool for a few minutes before slicing in half lengthwise and scooping out the flesh.
Mash the potato flesh with a fork, then stir in the heavy cream, shredded cheese, chopped hard-boiled egg, chopped cilantro, salt, and black pepper.
Spoon the potato mixture back into the potato skins, and top each with a spoonful of the nimono-simmer.
Return the potatoes to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the tops are lightly browned.
Serve the causa limena hot, garnished with additional chopped cilantro if desired.
